name: Texas DSHS Asbestos License Search

agency.name: Texas Department of State Health Services

updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the DSHS online licensing search and asbestos roster pages before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: Texas asbestos abatement contractor licenses and registrations exposed by DSHS online licensing paths
Other asbestos program license and registration categories exposed by DSHS search and roster pages
knownExclusions: This source is scoped to Texas DSHS asbestos-program licenses and registrations, especially asbestos-related activities in public buildings.
It is not a statewide general contractor, demolition-permit, local building-permit, or non-asbestos construction-license source.
The online search and roster paths have not been adapter-reviewed for stable machine-readable access, completeness, privacy fields, or redistribution terms.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Use the official search and roster pages manually until a stable public export contract and access constraints are reviewed.
publicRecordsNotes: Official asbestos-program source metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that an asbestos credential, notification, permit, or local authorization does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: The DSHS asbestos find-a-licensee page references the online licensing database and PDF rosters. No stable machine-readable bulk contract has been adapter-reviewed.
researchNotes: DSHS states that persons and companies engaging in asbestos-related activities in a public building in Texas must be licensed or registered by DSHS. This source remains a manual handoff until the lookup and roster paths are reviewed for stable, lawful automation.
verificationCaveats: This source covers DSHS asbestos-program credentials, not general contractor or local permit records.
No matching record means no match in this source at the checked time, not proof that an asbestos credential, notification, permit, or local authorization does not exist elsewhere.
verificationNotes: No supported sync or verification adapter is exposed while the official search and roster shapes remain under review.
maintainerNotes: Keep public-building asbestos scope explicit. Do not publish roster/search records without source-specific privacy and redistribution review.

researchEvidence.note: Official DSHS licensing page states persons and companies engaging in asbestos-related activities in Texas public buildings must be appropriately licensed or registered by DSHS.
Official DSHS find-a-licensee page points users to the online licensing database and PDF rosters for asbestos professionals.
Official DSHS online licensing page lists asbestos abatement contractor, worker, consultant agency, and related asbestos categories as eligible for online licensing paths.
Official DSHS site policies page reviewed for policy posture; redistribution rights remain unconfirmed.

blocker.summary: The official path is lookup and roster oriented, and no stable machine-readable data contract, completeness statement, or reuse position has been adapter-reviewed.
